student_inputs.project_summary: I built a Secure Secrets Manager using Python Flask following DevSecOps principles. The application supports user registration and login with JWT authentication, encrypted storage of sensitive secrets using Fernet, owner-only access control, secret creation, retrieval, listing, metadata update, deletion, and one-time expiring share links. Passwords are securely hashed, sensitive configuration is loaded from environment variables, rate limiting is used to reduce abuse, and JSON file storage using Python open() is used as required by the assignment. I also added tests for authentication, secrets management, and sharing, and the test files passed successfully. A key challenge was resolving the requirement conflict between file-based storage and database migrations, and the final architecture intentionally uses file-based persistence because that was explicitly required.
student_inputs.ai_interaction_summary: I used ChatGPT as an AI coding assistant throughout the project. I first sent the full assignment requirements and asked the AI to build the project like a professional DevSecOps engineer. The AI generated the first project structure, Flask code, encryption logic, JWT authentication, file-based storage, routes, tests, and documentation. I reviewed the AI output and found missing files such as .env, secrets_data JSON files, __init__.py files, and missing test files. I asked the AI to correct those. I also asked why VSCode imports were failing, and the AI explained virtual environments, dependencies, and interpreter selection. I then asked the AI to audit the full requirements, and it identified missing or partial parts such as authentication middleware, share ownership validation, stronger validation, audit logging, rate limiting, and documentation. I challenged the AI about whether a database file was needed, and we clarified that the project should remain file-based because the assignment required open() storage. I requested standalone API documentation and a corrected AI interaction log. Finally, I ran the generated tests and confirmed that conftest.py, test_auth.py, test_secrets.py, and test_sharing.py worked successfully. This shows that I used AI interactively: I prompted it, reviewed its work, corrected it, asked architectural questions, tested the output, and improved the final project through the conversation.
